steps.title: Map the passage structure
steps.content: The narrator begins with a broad claim: in a boat, each crew member thinks he is doing everything. He then gives Harris and George as examples of that pattern.

steps.title: Match general claim to examples
steps.content: A says the situation is unexpected, B centers the narrator's role, and C suggests shared contribution to a challenge. Harris thinks he alone worked, and George thinks he did all the labor worth mentioning; those examples support the narrator's opening observation. The passage instead presents a generalization and illustrates it with a specific situation. The answer is D.
